duas.arabic: أَسْتَـوْدِعُكُـمُ اللَّهَ الَّذي لا تَضـيعُ وَدائِعُـه
duas.transliteration: Astawdi`ukumu ‘llāha ‘l-ladhi lā taḍī`u wadā'i`uh.
duas.translation: I place you in the trust of Allah, whose trust is never misplaced. Reference: Ahmad 2/403, Ibn Majah 2/943. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 2/133.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Supplication of the resident for the traveller

duas.arabic: أَسْتَـوْدِعُ اللَّهَ ديـنَكَ وَأَمانَتَـكَ، وَخَـواتيـمَ عَمَـلِك
duas.transliteration: Astawdi`ullāha dīnak, wa amānatak, wa khawātīma `amalik.
duas.translation: I leave your religion in the care of Allah, as well as your safety, and the last of your deeds. Reference: Ahmad 2/7, At-Tirmidhi 5/499. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/155.

duas.arabic: زَوَّدَكَ اللَّهُ التقْوى، وَغَفَـرَذَنْـبَكَ، وَيَسَّـرَ لَكَ الخَـيْرَ حَيْـثُما كُنْـت
duas.transliteration: Zawwadaka ‘llāhut-taqwā, wa ghafara dhanbak, wa yassara laka ‘l-khayra ḥaythu mā kunt.
duas.translation: May Allah give you piety as your provision, forgive your sins, and make goodness easy for you wherever you are. Reference: At-Tirmidhi. See Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi, 3/155.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Takbir and Tasbih during travel

duas.arabic: قَالَ جَابِرٌ رضي الله عنه: (كُنَّا إِذَا صَعَدْنَا كَبَّرْنَا، وَإِذَا نَزَلْنَا سَبَّحْنَا)
duas.transliteration: Qāla Jābirun (raḍiyallāhu ‘anhu): Kunnā idhā ṣa‘adnā kabbarnā, wa idhā nazalnā sabbaḥnā.
duas.translation:
Jabir (ra) said: "Whenever we went up a hill we would say 'Allāhu Akbar' (Allah is the Most Great) and when we descended we would say 'Subḥānallāh' (Glory is to Allah)."

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/135.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Prayer of the traveller as dawn approaches

duas.arabic: سَمِـعَ سـامِعٌ بِحَمْـدِ اللهِ وَحُسْـنِ بَلائِـهِ عَلَيْـنا. رَبَّنـا صـاحِبْـنا وَأَفْـضِل عَلَيْـنا عائِذاً باللهِ مِنَ النّـار
duas.transliteration: Sami‘a sāmi‘un bi-ḥamdillāhi wa ḥusni balā’ihi ‘alaynā. Rabbanā ṣāḥibnā, wa afḍil ‘alaynā, ‘ā’idhan billāhi mina-n-nār.
duas.translation:
He Who listens has heard that we praise Allah for the good things He gives us. Our Lord, be with us and bestow Your favor upon us. I seek the protection of Allah from the Fire.

Reference: Muslim 4/2086, the meaning of sami`a sāmi`un (who listens has heard) is that 'a witness has witnessed our praise of Allah due to His blessings and favor upon us.' It could also be read samma`a sāmi`un, in which case it means 'one who has heard this statement of mine will convey it to another and he will say it as well.' This is due to the attention given to the Thikr (remembrance of Allah) and supplications made during the early morning hours. The meaning of his saying 'Our Lord, be with us and bestow Your favor upon us' is: 'Our Lord, protect us and guard us. Bless us with Your numerous bounties, and avert from us every evil.' See An-Nawawi, Sharh Sahih Muslim 17/39.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Stopping or lodging somewhere in travel and otherwise
chapters.title_arabic: الدعاء إذا نزل منزلا في سفر أو غيره

duas.arabic: أَعـوذُ بِكَلِـماتِ اللّهِ التّـامّاتِ مِنْ شَـرِّ ما خَلَـق
duas.transliteration: A‘ūdhu bi-kalimātillāhi-t-tāmmāti min sharri mā khalaq.
duas.translation:
I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.

Reference: Muslim 4/2080.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: While returning from travel

duas.arabic: يُكَبِّرُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَرَفٍ ثَلاَثَ تَكْبِيرَاتٍ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، آيِبُونَ، تَائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنا حَامِدُونَ، صَدَقَ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزَابَ وَحْدَهُ
duas.transliteration: Yukabbiru ‘alā kulli sharafin thalātha takbīrātin thumma yaqūlu: Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahul-mulku, wa lahul-ḥamdu, wa huwa ‘alā kulli shay’in qadīr, ā’ibūna, tā’ibūna, ‘ābidūna, li-Rabbinā ḥāmidūna, ṣadaqallāhu wa‘dah, wa naṣara ‘abdah, wa hazama-l-aḥzāba waḥdah.
duas.translation:
From every elevated point say 'Allāhu Akbar' three times and then recite: "None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. We return repentant to our Lord, worshipping our Lord, and praising our Lord. He fulfilled His Promise, He aided His slave, and He alone defeated the Confederates."

Reference: Bukhari 7/163, Muslim 2/980. The Prophet (ﷺ) used to say this when returning from a campaign or from Hajj.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: What to say upon receiving pleasing or displeasing news
chapters.title_arabic: ما يقول من أتاه أمر يسره أو يكرهه

duas.arabic: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَسُرُّهُ قَالَ: (الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ) وَإِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَكْرَهُهُ قَالَ: (الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ)
duas.transliteration: Kāna an-Nabiyyu (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam) idhā atāhu-l-amru yasurruhu qāl: (Al-ḥamdu lillāhilladhī bi-ni‘matihi tatimmuṣ-ṣāliḥāt). Wa idhā atāhu-l-amru yakrahuhu qāl: (Al-ḥamdu lillāhi ‘alā kulli ḥāl).
duas.translation:
When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet (ﷺ) used to say: "Praise is to Allah Who by His blessings all good things are perfected." And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say: "Praise is to Allah in all circumstances."

Reference: Ibn As-Sunni, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah, and Al-Hakim who graded it authentic 1/499. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 4/201.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Excellence of sending prayers upon the Prophet (saws)

duas.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (مَنْ صَلَّى عَلَيَّ صَلاَةً صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ بِهَا عَشْراً)
duas.transliteration: Qāla an-Nabiyyu (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam): Man ṣallā ‘alayya ṣalātan ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi bihā ‘ashrā.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Whoever prays for Allah's blessings upon me once, will be blessed for it by Allah ten times."

Reference: Muslim 1/288.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (لاَ تَجْعَلُوا قَبْرِي عِيداً وَصَلُّوا عَلَيَّ؛ فَإِنَّ صَلاَتَكُم تَبْلُغُنِي حَيْثُ كُنْتُمْ)
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Lā taj‘alū qabrī ‘īdan, wa ṣallū ‘alayya; fa-inna ṣalātakum tablughunī ḥaythu kuntum.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Do not make my grave a place of ritual celebration, but pray for Allah's blessings upon me, for your blessings reach me from wherever you are."

Reference: Abu Dawud 2/218, Ahmad 2/367. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 2/383.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (الْبَخِيلُ مَنْ ذُكِرْتُ عِنْدَهُ فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ عَلَيَّ)
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Al-bakhīlu man dhukirtu ‘indahu falam yuṣalli ‘alayya.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "The miser is the one in whose presence I am mentioned yet does not pray for Allah's blessings upon me."

Reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/551 and others. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/177 and Sahihul-Jarai' As-Saghir 3/25.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم:(إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَلاَئِكَةً سَيَّاحِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يُبَلِّغُونِي مِنْ أُمَّتِي السَّلاَمَ)
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Inna lillāhi malā’ikatan sayyāḥīna fi-l-arḍi yuballighūnī min ummatī-s-salām.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Indeed Allah has angels who roam the earth and they convey to me the greetings (or prayers of peace) of my Ummah (nation)."

Reference: An-Nasa'i, Al-Hakim 2/421. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih An-Nasa'i 1/274.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يُسَلِّمُ عَلَيَّ إِلاَّ رَدَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ رُوحِيَ حَتَّى أَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمَ)
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Mā min aḥadin yusallimu ‘alayya illā radda-llāhu ‘alayya rūḥiya ḥattā arudda ‘alayhi-s-salām.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "No one sends greetings (or prayers of peace) upon me but Allah returns my soul to me so that I may return his greetings."

Reference: Abu Dawud (no. 2041). Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/383.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Excellence of spreading the Islamic greeting

duas.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (لاَ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى تُؤْمِنُوا، وَلاَ تُؤْمِنُوا حَتَّى تَحَابُّوا، أَوَلاَ أَدُلُّكُم عَلَى شَيْءٍ إِذَا فَعَلْتُمُوهُ تَحَابَبْتُم، أَفْشُوا السَّلاَمَ بَيْنَكُمْ)
duas.transliteration: Qāla Rasūlullāhi (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam): Lā tadkhulū-l-jannata ḥattā tu’minū, wa lā tu’minū ḥattā taḥābbū. Awalā adullukum ‘alā shay’in idhā fa‘altumūhu taḥābabtum? Afshū-s-salāma baynakum.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "You shall not enter Paradise until you believe, and you have not believed until you love one another. Shall I tell you of something you can do to make you love one another? Spread the greetings of Salam (peace) amongst yourselves."

Reference: Muslim 1/74 and others.

duas.arabic: ثَلاَثٌ مَنْ جَمَعَهُنَّ فَقَدْ جَمَعَ الْإِيمَانَ: الْإِنْصَافُ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ، وَبَذْلُ السَّلاَمِ لِلْعَالَمِ، وَالْإِنْفَاقُ مِنَ الإِقْتَارِ
duas.transliteration: Thalāthun man jama‘ahunna faqad jama‘a-l-īmān: Al-inṣāfu min nafsik, wa badhlu-s-salāmi lil-‘ālam, wal-infāqu mina-l-iqtār.
duas.translation:
Three characteristics, whoever combines them, has completed his faith: to be just, to spread greetings to all people, and to spend (charitably) out of the little you have.

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 1/82 as a statement of the Companion 'Ammar (RA).

duas.arabic: وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: أنَّ رَجُلاً سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أيُّ الْإِسْلاَمِ خَيْرٌ قَالَ: (تُطْعِمُ الطَّعَامَ، وَتَقْرأُ السَّلاَمَ عَلَى مَنْ عَرَفْتَ وَمَنْ لَمْ تَعْرِفْ)
duas.transliteration: Wa ‘an ‘Abdillāhi bni ‘Umara (raḍiyallāhu ‘anhumā): Anna rajulan sa’ala-n-Nabiyya (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam): Ayyu-l-islāmi khayr? Qāl: (Tuṭ‘imu-ṭ-ṭa‘ām, wa taqra’u-s-salāma ‘alā man ‘arafta wa man lam ta‘rif).
duas.translation:
`Abdullah bin `Umar (RA) said: A man asked the Prophet (ﷺ), "What is the best act of Islam?" He said, "To feed others and to give greetings of Salam (peace) to those whom you know and to those whom you do not know."

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 1/55, Muslim 1/65.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Returning a greeting to a disbeliever
chapters.title_arabic: كيف يرد السلام على الكافر إذا سلم

duas.arabic: إذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ فَقُولُوا: وَعَلَيْكُمْ
duas.transliteration: Idhā sallama ‘alaykum ahlu-l-kitābi fa-qūlū: Wa ‘alaykum.
duas.translation:
If one of the People of the Scripture (i.e. Christians and Jews) greets you, saying 'As-Salāmu `alaykum', then say (to him): "Wa `alaykum" (And upon you).

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/42, Muslim 4/1705.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Upon hearing a rooster crow or the braying of a donkey
chapters.title_arabic: دعاء عند سماع صياح الديك ونهيق الحمار

duas.arabic: إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ صِيَاحَ الدِّيَكَةِ فَاسْأَلُوا اللَّهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ؛ فَإِنَّهَا رَأَتْ مَلَكاً وَإِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نَهِيقَ الْحِمَارِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ؛ فَإِنَّهُ رَأَى شَيْطَاناً
duas.transliteration: Idhā sami‘tum ṣiyāḥa-d-diyakati fas’alullāha min faḍlih; fa-innahā ra’at malakan. Wa idhā sami‘tum nahīqa-l-ḥimāri fata‘awwadhū billāhi mina-sh-shayṭān; fa-innahu ra’ā shayṭānan.
duas.translation:
When you hear the cock's crow, ask Allah for His favor upon you for surely it has seen an angel. When you hear the bray of a donkey, seek refuge in Allah from Satan, for surely it has seen a devil.

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/350, Muslim 4/2092.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Upon hearing the barking of dogs at night
chapters.title_arabic: دعاء عند سماع نباح الكلاب بالليل

duas.arabic: إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نُبَاحَ الْكِلاَبِ وَنَهِيقَ الْحَمِيرِ بِاللَّيْلِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنْهُنَّ؛ فَإِنَّهُنَّ يَرَيْنَ مَا لاَ تَرَوْنَ
duas.transliteration: Idhā sami‘tum nubāḥa-l-kilābi wa nahīqa-l-ḥamīri bil-layli fata‘awwadhū billāhi minhunn; fa-innahunna yarayna mā lā tarawn.
duas.translation:
When you hear a dog barking or a donkey braying in the night, then seek refuge in Allah from them, for surely they have seen what you see not.

Reference: Abu Dawud 4/327, Ahmad 3/306. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 3/961.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: For one you have insulted

duas.arabic: قال صلى الله عليه وسلم: "اللَّهُم فأيما مؤمن سببته فاجعل ذلك له قربة إليك يوم القيامة"
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma fa-ayyumā mu’minin sababtuhu faj‘al dhālika lahu qurbatan ilayka yawma-l-qiyāmah.
duas.translation:
O Allah, whomever of the believers I have abused, make that for him a source of nearness to You on the Day of Resurrection.

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/171, Muslim 4/2007. The wording in Muslim's report is: 'make it a purification for him and a source of mercy.'

chapters.title_english: Chapter: The etiquette of praising a fellow Muslim
chapters.title_arabic: ما يقول المسلم إذا مدح المسلم

duas.arabic: قال صلى الله عليه وسلم: "إِذَا كَانَ أَحَدُكُم مَادِحاً صَاحِبَهُ لاَ مَحَالَةَ فَلْيَقُلْ: أَحْسِبُ فُلاَناً وَاللَّهُ حَسِيبُهُ، وَلاَ أُزَكِّي عَلَى اللَّهِ أَحَداً، أَحْسِبُهُ – إِنْ كَانَ يَعْلَمُ ذَاكَ – كَذَا وَكَذَا"
duas.transliteration: Qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Idhā kāna aḥadukum mādiḥan ṣāḥibahu lā maḥālata fal-yaqul: Aḥsibu fulānan wallāhu ḥasībuh, wa lā uzakkī ‘alallāhi aḥada, aḥsibuhu—in kāna ya‘lamu dhāka—kadhā wa kadhā.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "If any of you must praise his companion, let him say: 'I consider so-and-so [to be such], and Allah is his Assessor, and I do not prize anyone above Allah. I consider him—if he knows that of him—to be such and such.'"

Reference: Muslim 4/2296.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: For the one that have been praised

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا يَقُولُونَ وَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ [وَاجْعَلْنِي خَيْرًا مِمَّا يَظُّنُّونَ]
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma lā tu'ākhidhnī bimā yaqūlūn, waghfir lī mā lā ya`lamūn [waj`alnī khayran mimmā yaẓunnūn].
duas.translation: O Allah, do not call me to account for what they say and forgive me for what they have no knowledge of [and make me better than they imagine]. Reference: Al-Bukhari, Al-'Adabul-Mufrad no. 761. See Al-Albani, Sahih Al-'Adabul-Mufrad (no. 585). The portion between brackets if from Al-Bayhaqi, Shu'ab Al-Iman 4/228, and comes another account.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: The Talbiya for the one doing Hajj or 'Umrah
chapters.title_arabic: كيف يلبي المحرم في الحج أو العمرة

duas.arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ والنِّعْمَة لَكَ والمُلْكُ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
duas.transliteration: Labbayk-Allāhumma labbayk, labbayka lā sharīka laka labbayk, inna ‘l-ḥamda, wanni`mata, laka wa ‘l-mulk, lā sharīka lak.
duas.translation: I am here at Your service, O Allah, I am here at Your service. I am here at Your service, You have no partner, I am here at Your service. Surely the praise, and blessings are Yours, and the dominion. You have no partner. Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 3/408, Muslim 2/841.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: The Takbîr passing the black stone

duas.arabic: طَافَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ كُلَّمَا أَتَى الرُّكْنَ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ بِشَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ وَكَبَّرَ
duas.transliteration: Ṭāfa an-Nabiyyu (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam) bil-bayti ‘alā ba‘īrin, kullamā ata-r-rukna ashāra ilayhi bi-shay’in ‘indahu wa kabbara.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) performed Tawaf riding a camel. Every time he passed the corner (containing the Black Stone), he would point to it with something that he was holding and say: 'Allāhu Akbar' (Allah is the Most Great)!

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 3/476. See also 472. The 'something' that was referred to in this Hadith was a cane.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Between the Yemeni corner and the black stone
chapters.title_arabic: الدعاء بين الركن اليماني والحجر الأسود

duas.arabic: ﴿رَبَّنَا آتِنَا في الدُّنْيَا حسَنَةً وفي الآخِرَةِ حسَنةً وقِنَا عذَابَ النَّارِ﴾
duas.transliteration: Rabbanā ātinā fid-dunyā ḥasanah, wa fil-ākhirati ḥasanah, wa qinā ‘adhāban-nār.
duas.translation:
Our Lord, grant us the good things in this world, and the good things in the next life, and save us from the punishment of the Fire.

Reference: Abu Dawud 2/179, Ahmad 3/411, Al-Baghawi, Sharhus-Sunnah 7/128. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/354. The Ayat is from Surat Al-Baqarah, 2:201.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: When at Mount Safa and Mount Marwah

duas.arabic: (لَمَّا دَنَا النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مِنَ الصَّفَا قَرَأَ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَآئِرِ اللَّهِ﴾ "أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ" فَبَدَأَ بِالصَّفَا فَرَقِيَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى رَأَى الْبَيْتَ، فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ، فَوَحَّدَ اللَّهَ وَكبَّرَهُ وَقَالَ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ، أَنْجَزَ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزَابَ وَحْدَهُ، ثُمَّ دَعَا بَيْنَ ذلكَ. قَالَ مِثْلَ هَذَا ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ) الْحَدِيثُ. وَفِيهِ: (فَفَعَلَ عَلَى الْمَرْوَةِ كَمَا فَعَلَ عَلَى الصَّفَا)
duas.transliteration: Lammā danā an-Nabiyyu (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam) minaṣ-Ṣafā qara’a: (Innaṣ-Ṣafā wal-Marwata min sha‘ā’irillāh). 'Abda’u bimā bada’allāhu bih'. Fa bada’a biṣ-Ṣafā faraqiya ‘alayhi ḥattā ra’al-bayt, fastaqbala-l-qiblah, fawaḥḥadallāha wa kabbarahu wa qāl: (Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-ḥamdu wa huwa ‘alā kulli shay’in qadīr. Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu, anjaza wa‘dah, wa naṣara ‘abdah, wa hazama-l-aḥzāba waḥdah). Thumma da‘ā bayna dhālik. Qāla mithla hādhā thalātha marrāt.
duas.translation:
Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) approached Mount Safa, he would recite: 'Surely Safa and Marwah are among the signs of Allah. I begin by that which Allah began.' He began (his Sa'y) at Mount Safa climbing it until he could see the House. He then faced the Qiblah, declaring the Oneness of Allah and His greatness, saying: 'There is none worthy of worship but Allah alone, Who has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, He fulfilled His Promise, He aided His slave, and He alone defeated the Confederates.' Then he would supplicate, repeating this three times. He did at Mount Marwah as he did at Mount Safa.

Reference: Muslim 2/888.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: The Day of 'Arafah

duas.arabic: خير الدعاء دعاء يوم عرفة ، وخيرُ ما قلت أنا والنبيُّون من قبلي : لا إله إلا الله وحدهُ لا شريك لهُ ، لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحمدُ وهو على كل شيء قدير.
duas.transliteration: Khayru-d-du‘ā’i du‘ā’u yawmi ‘Arafah, wa khayru mā qultu ana wan-nabiyyūna min qablī: Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-ḥamdu wa huwa ‘alā kulli shay’in qadīr.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "The best invocation is that of the Day of Arafat, and the best that anyone can say is what I and the Prophets before me have said: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah Alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things."

Reference: At-Tirmidhi. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/184, and also Silsilatul-'Ahadith As-Sahihah 4/6.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Remembrance at Muzdalifa

duas.arabic: رَكِبَ صلى الله عليه وسلم الْقَصْوَاءَ حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَشْعَرَ الْحَرَامَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ (فَدَعَاهُ، وَكَبَّرَهُ، وَهَللَّهُ، وَوَحَّدَهُ) فَلَمْ يَزَلْ وَاقِفاً حَتَّى أَسْفَرَ جِدَّاً فَدَفَعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْلُعَ الشَّمسُ.
duas.transliteration: Rakiba (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam) al-Qaṣwā’a ḥattā ata-l-Mash‘ara-l-Ḥarām fastaqbala-l-qiblah (fada‘āhu, wa kabbarahu, wa hallalahu, wa waḥḥadahu) falam yazal wāqifan ḥattā asfara jiddan fadafa‘a qabla an taṭlu‘a-sh-shams.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) rode Al-Qaswa until he reached Al-Mash'ar Al-Haram, he then faced the Qiblah, supplicated to Allah, and extoled His greatness, oneness and declared that none is worthy of worship but Him. He stood until the light of morning was clear but left before the sun rose.

Reference: Muslim 2/891.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Takbir when throwing each pebble at the Jamarat
chapters.title_arabic: التكبير عند رمي الجمار مع كل حصاة

duas.arabic: يُكَبِّرُ كُلَّمَا رَمَى بِحَصَاةٍ عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ الثَّلاَثِ، ثُمَّ يَتَقَدَّمُ، ويَقِفُ يَدْعُو مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبلَةِ، رَافِعاً يَدَيْهِ بَعْدَ الْجَمْرَةِ الْأُولَى وَالثَّانِيَةِ. أَمَّا جَمْرَةُ الْعَقَبَةِ فَيَرْمِيهَا وَيُكَبِّرُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ حصاة وَيَنْصَرِفُ وَلاَ يَقِفُ عِنْدَهَا
duas.transliteration: Yukabbiru kullamā ramā bi-ḥaṣātin ‘inda-l-jimāri-th-thalāth, thumma yataqaddamu, wa yaqifu yad‘ū mustaqbila-l-qiblah, rāfi‘an yadayhi ba‘da-l-jamrati-l-ūlā wath-thāniyah. Ammā jamratul-‘aqabah fayarmīhā wa yukabbiru ‘inda kulli ḥaṣātin wa yanṣarifu wa lā yaqifu ‘indahā.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ) said 'Allāhu Akbar' (Allah is the Most Great) with each pebble he threw at the three pillars. Then he went forward, stood facing the Qiblah, and raised his hands and called upon Allah. That was after (stoning) the first and second pillars. As for the third (Jamratul-Aqabah), he stoned it and said 'Allāhu Akbar' with every pebble he threw, but when he was finished he left without standing at it for supplication.

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 3/581, 3, 4, and Muslim.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: At times of amazement and that which delights
chapters.title_arabic: ما يقول عند التعجب والأمر السار

duas.arabic: سُـبْحانَ الله
duas.transliteration: Subḥānallāh!
duas.translation:
Glory is to Allah.

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 1/210, 390, 414 and Muslim 4/1857.

duas.arabic: اللهُ أَكْـبَر
duas.transliteration: Allāhu Akbar!
duas.translation:
Allah is the Most Great.

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 8/441. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/103, 235, Ahmad 5/218.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Upon receiving pleasant news
chapters.title_arabic: ما يفعل من أتاه أمر يسره

duas.arabic: كَانَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ أَمْرٌ يَسُرُّهُ أَوْ يُسَرُّ بِهِ خَرَّ سَاجِداً شُكْراً لِلَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى
duas.transliteration: Kāna an-Nabiyyu (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallama) idhā atāhu amrun yasurruhu aw yusarru bihi kharra sāji-dan shukran lillāhi Tabāraka wa Ta‘ālā.
duas.translation:
The Prophet (ﷺ), upon receiving news which pleased him or which caused pleasure, would prostrate in gratitude to Allah blessed and exalted.

Reference: Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, At-Tirmidhi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/233, and 'Irwa'ul-Ghalil 2/226.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: When feeling some pain in the body
chapters.title_arabic: ما يفعل ويقول من أحس وجعاً في جسده

duas.arabic: ضَعْ يَدَكَ عَلَى الَّذِي تَألَّمَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ وَقُلْ: "بِسْمِ اللَّهِ" ثَلاَثاً، وَقُلْ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ: "أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ"
duas.transliteration: Ḍa‘ yadaka ‘ala-lladhī ta’allama min jasadika wa qul: (Bismillāh) thalāthan, wa qul sab‘a marrāt: (A‘ūdhu billāhi wa qudratihi min sharri mā ajidu wa uḥādhir).
duas.translation:
Put your hand on the place where you feel pain and say: 'With the Name of Allah' (three times). Then say seven times: 'I seek refuge in Allah and in His Power from the evil of what I find and of what I guard against.'

Reference: Muslim 4/1728.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: When in fear of afflicting something with an (evil) eye from oneself
chapters.title_arabic: ما يقول من خشي أن يصيب شيئاً بعينه

duas.arabic: إِذَا رَأَى أَحَدُكُم مِنْ أَخِيهِ، أَوْ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ، أَوْ مِنْ مَالِهِ مَا يُعْجِبُهُ [فَلْيَدْعُ لَهُ بِالْبَرَكَةِ] فَإِنَّ الْعَيْنَ حَقٌّ
duas.transliteration: Idhā ra’ā aḥadukum min akhīhi, aw min nafsihi, aw min mālihi mā yu‘jibuhu [falyad‘u lahu bil-barakah] fa-inna al-‘ayna ḥaqq.
duas.translation:
If you see something from your brother, yourself or wealth which you find impressing, then invoke blessings for it, for the evil eye is indeed true.

Reference: Ahmad 4/447, Ibn Majah, Malik. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 1/212. Also see Al-Arna'ut's checking of Ibn Al-Qayyim's Zadul-Ma'ad 4/170.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: When startled

duas.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ
duas.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāh!
duas.translation: There is none worthy of worship but Allah! Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/181, Muslim 4/2208.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: When slaughtering or offering a sacrifice
chapters.title_arabic: ما يقول عند الذبح أو النحر

duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ [اللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَلَكَ] اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي
duas.transliteration: Bismillāh wallāhu Akbar [Allāhumma minka wa lak] Allāhumma taqabbal minnī.
duas.translation: With the Name of Allah, Allah is the Most Great! [O Allah, from You and to You.] O Allah, accept it from me. Reference: Muslim 3/ 1557, Al-Bayhaqi 9/287.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: To ward off the plot of the rebellious devils
chapters.title_arabic: ما يقول لرد كيد مردة الشياطين

duas.arabic: أَعُوذُ بكَلِمَاتِ اللهِ التَّامَّاتِ الَّتِي لَا يُجَاوِزُهُنَّ بَرٌّ ولَا فَاجرٌ مِنْ شّرِّ مَا خَلقَ، وبَرَأَ وذَرَأَ، ومِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ وِمنْ شَرِّ مَا يَعْرُجُ فيهَا، ومِن شَرِّ مَا ذَرَأَ في الأَرْضِ ومِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا، وِمنْ شَرِّ فِتَنِ اللَّيْلِ والنَّهارِ، ومِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ طارِقٍ إِلَّا طَارِقاً يَطْرُقُ بخَيْرٍ يَا رَحْمَنُ
duas.transliteration: A`ūdhu bikalimāti ‘llāhit-tāmmāti ‘llatī lā yujāwizuhunna barrun wa lā fājirun min sharri mā khalaq, wa bara'a wa dhara', wa min sharri mā yanzilu minas-samā', wa min sharri mā ya`ruju fīhā, wa min sharri mā dhara'a fi ‘l-arḍ, wa min sharri ma yakhruju minhā, wa min sharri fitani ‘llayli wannahār, wa min sharri kulli ṭāriqin illā ṭāriqan yaṭruqu bikhayr yā Rahmān.
duas.translation: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah -which neither the upright nor the corrupt may overcome - from the evil of what He created, of what He made, and of what He scattered, from the evil of what descends from the heavens, and of what rises up to them, from the evil of what He scattered in the earth, and of what emerges from it, from the evil trials of night and day, and from the evil of every night visitor, except the night visitor who comes with good. O Merciful One. Reference: Ahmad 3/419, with an authentic chain of narration, and Ibn As-Sunni (no. 637). Al-Arna'ut, graded its chain authentic in his checking of Al-'Aqidah At-Tahawiyyah p. 133. See also Majma'uz-Zawa'id, 10/127.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Seeking forgiveness and repentance

duas.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: "وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لأَسْتَغفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ فِي الْيَوْمِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةٍ"
duas.transliteration: Qāla Rasūlullāhi ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Wallāhi innī la-astaghfirullāha wa atūbu ilayhi fī-l-yawmi akthara min sab‘īna marrah.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "By Allah, I seek the forgiveness of Allah, and repent to Him more than seventy times in a day."

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/101.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَإِنِّي أَتُوبُ فِي الْيَوْمِ إِلَيْهِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Yā ayyuha-n-nāsu tūbū ilallāhi fa-innī atūbu fī-l-yawmi ilayhi mi’ata marrah.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "O people, repent to Allah, for I verily repent to Him one hundred times a day."

Reference: Muslim 4/2076.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: مَنْ قَالَ (أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ القَيّوُمُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيهِ)، غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَإِنْ كَانَ فَرَّ مِنَ الزَّحْفِ.
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Man qāla (Astaghfirullāha-l-‘Aẓīm alladhī lā ilāha illā huwa-l-Ḥayyu-l-Qayyūmu wa atūbu ilayh), ghafara-llāhu lahu wa in kāna farra mina-z-zaḥf.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "Whoever says: 'I seek the forgiveness of Allah the Mighty, Whom there is none worthy of worship except Him, the Living, the Eternal, and I repent to Him,' Allah will forgive him even if he has deserted the army's ranks."

Reference: Abu Dawud 2/85, At-Tirmidhi 5/569, and Al-Hakim who declared it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him 1/511. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/182. See also Jami'ul-'Usool li-'Ahdaith Ar-Rasool 4/ 389-90 checked by Al-Arna'ut.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الرَّبُّ مِنَ الْعَبْدِ فِي جَوْفِ اللَّيْلِ الآخِرِ فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِمَّنْ يَذْكُرُ اللَّهَ فِي تِلْكَ السَّاعَةِ فَكُنْ
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Aqrabu mā yakūnu-r-Rabbu mina-l-‘abdi fī jawfi-l-layli-l-ākhir, fa-ini-staṭa‘ta an takūna mimmān yadhkurullāha fī tilka-s-sā‘ati fakun.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "The closest that the Lord comes to the slave is in the last portion of the night. So, if you are able to be among those who remember Allah in this hour, then be among them."

Reference: At-Tirmidhi, An-Nasa'i 1/279 and Al-Hakim. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/183, and Jdmi'ul-'Usool with Al-Arna'ut's checking 4/144.

duas.arabic: .وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الْعَبْدُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَهُوَ سَاجِدٌ فَأَكثِرُوا الدُّعَاءَ.
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Aqrabu mā yakūnu-l-‘abdu min Rabbihi wa huwa sājidun, fa-akthirū-d-du‘ā’.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "The closest that the slave comes to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so invoke Allah much (in prostration)."

Reference: Muslim 1/350.

duas.arabic: .وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: إِنَّهُ لَيُغَانُ عَلَى قَلْبِي وَإِنِّي لأَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ فِي الْيَوْمِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ.
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Innahu layughānu ‘alā qalbī wa innī la-astaghfirullāha fī-l-yawmi mi’ata marrah.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "Sometimes I perceive a cloud over my heart, and I seek Allah's forgiveness a hundred times a day."

Reference: Muslim 4/2075. Ibn 'Athir explains that the Prophet (ﷺ) was always vigilant in his remembrance and drawing near to Allah, and if he forgot to do any of what he normally did from time to time, or it slipped his mind, he felt as if he had wronged himself and so he would begin to seek the forgiveness of Allah. See Jami'ul-'Usool 4/386.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Excellence of Tasbih, Tahmid, Tahlil, and Takbir
chapters.title_arabic: من فضل التسبيح والتحميد ، والتهليل ، والتكبير

duas.arabic: قَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: مَنْ قَالَ (سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ) فِي يَوْمٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ حُطَّتْ خَطَايَاهُ وَلَوْ كَانَتْ مِثْلَ زَبَدِ الْبَحْر.
duas.transliteration: Qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Man qāla (Subḥānallāhi wa bi-ḥamdihi) fī yawmin mi’ata marrah, ḥuṭṭat khaṭāyāhu wa law kānat mithla zabadi-l-baḥr.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "Whoever says: 'Glorified is Allah and praised is He' one hundred times a day, will have his sins forgiven even if they are like the foam of the sea."

Reference: Al-Bukhari 7/168, Muslim 4/2071, see also invocation no. 91 of this book.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: مَنْ قَالَ (لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ) عَشْرَ مِرَارٍ، كَانَ كَمَنْ أَعْتَقَ أَرْبَعَةَ أَنْفُسٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Man qāla (Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-ḥamdu wa huwa ‘alā kulli shay’in qadīr) ‘ashra mirār, kāna kaman a‘taqa arba‘ata anfusin min waladi Ismā‘īl.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "Whoever says: 'None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things' ten times, will have the reward for freeing four slaves from the Children of Isma'il."

Reference: Al-Bukhari 7/67, Muslim 4/2071, see also invocation no. 93 of this book.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: كَلِمَتَانِ خَفِيفَتَانِ عَلَى اللِّسَانِ، ثَقِيلَتَانِ فِي الْمِيزَانِ، حَبِيبَتَانِ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ: (سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ)، (سُبْحانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ)
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Kalimatāni khafīfatāni ‘ala-l-lisān, thaqīlatāni fi-l-mīzān, ḥabībatāni ila-r-Raḥmān: (Subḥānallāhi wa bi-ḥamdihi), (Subḥānallāhi-l-‘Aẓīm).
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "Two words are light on the tongue, weigh heavily in the balance, and are loved by the Most Merciful One: 'Glorified is Allah and praised is He', 'Glorified is Allah the Most Great'."

Reference: Al-Bukhari 7/168, Muslim 4/2072.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: لَأَنْ أَقُولَ (سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ)، أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا طَلَعَتْ عَلَيْهِ الشَّمسُ.
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: La-an aqūla (Subḥānallāhi, wa-l-ḥamdulillāhi, wa lā ilāha illallāhu, wallāhu akbar), aḥabbu ilayya mimmā ṭala‘at ‘alayhi-sh-shams.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "For me to say: 'Glory is to Allah, and praise is to Allah, and there is none worthy of worship but Allah, and Allah is the Most Great' is dearer to me than all that the sun rises upon (i.e. the whole world)."

Reference: Muslim 4/2072.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُم أَنْ يَكْسِبَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ فَسَأَلَهُ سَائِلٌ مِنْ جُلَسَائِهِ كَيْفَ يَكْسِبُ أَحَدُنَا أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ قَالَ: يُسَبِّحُ مِائَةَ تَسْبِيحَةٍ، فَيُكتَبُ لَهُ أَلْفُ حَسَنَةٍ أَوْ يُحَطُّ عَنْهُ أَلْفُ خَطِيئَةٍ.
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: A-ya‘jizu aḥadukum an yaksiba kulla yawmin alfa ḥasanah? Fa sa’alahu sā’ilun min julasā’ihi: Kayfa yaksibu aḥadunā alfa ḥasanah? Qāla: Yusabbiḥu mi’ata tasbīḥah, fa yuktabu lahu alfu ḥasanatin aw yuḥaṭṭu ‘anhu alfu khaṭī’ah.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "Is anyone of you incapable of earning one thousand rewards in a day?" Someone from his gathering asked, "How can anyone of us earn a thousand rewards?" He said, "Glorify Allah a hundred times and a thousand rewards will be written for you, or a thousand sins will be wiped away."

Reference: Muslim 4/2073.

duas.arabic: مَنْ قَالَ (سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ) غُرِسَتْ لَهُ نَخْلَةٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ
duas.transliteration: Man qāla (Subḥānallāhi-l-‘Aẓīmi wa bi-ḥamdihi) ghurisat lahu nakhlatun fi-l-Jannah.
duas.translation:
Whoever says: 'Glorified is Allah the Most Great and praised is He', will have a date palm planted for him in Paradise.

Reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/511, and Al-Hakim who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 1/501. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 5/531 and Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/160.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ قَيْسٍ أَلاَ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى كَنْزٍ مِنْ كُنُوزِ الْجَنَّةِ؟ فَقُلْتُ: بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: قُلْ (لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ).
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Yā ‘Abdallāhi bna Qaysin, alā adulluka ‘alā kanzin min kunūzi-l-Jannah? Fa qultu: Balā yā Rasūlallāh. Qāla: Qul (Lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh).
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "O Abdullah bin Qais, should I not point you to one of the treasures of Paradise?" I said, "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." So he told me to say: 'There is no power and no might except by Allah'.

Reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/213, Muslim 4/2076.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: أَحَبُّ الْكَلاَمِ إِلَى اللَّهِ أَرْبَعٌ: (سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ)، وَ(الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ)، وَ(لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ)، وَ(اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ)، لاَ يَضُرُّكَ بِأَيِّهِنَّ بَدَأتَ
duas.transliteration: Wa qāla ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam: Aḥabbu-l-kalāmi ilallāhi arba‘: (Subḥānallāhi), wa (Al-ḥamdulillāhi), wa (Lā ilāha illallāhu), wa (Allāhu akbar), lā yaḍurruka bi-ayyihinna bada’t.
duas.translation:
Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "The most beloved words to Allah are four: 'Glorified is Allah', 'Praise is for Allah', 'There is none worthy of worship but Allah', and 'Allah is the Most Great'. It does not matter which one you start with."

Reference: Muslim 3/1685.

duas.arabic: جَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فَقَالَ: عَلِّمْنِي كَلاماً أَقُولُهُ: قَالَ: قُلْ (لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ كَبِيراً، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كَثِيراً، سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ رَبِّ العَالَمِينَ، لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ). قَالَ: فَهَؤُلاَءِ لِرَبِّي، فَمَا لِي؟ قَالَ: قُلْ (اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي).
duas.transliteration: Jā’a a‘rābiyyun ilā Rasūlillāhi ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallam fa qāla: ‘Allimnī kalāman aqūluhu. Qāla: Qul: (Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, Allāhu akbaru kabīra, wa-l-ḥamdulillāhi kathīra, Subḥānallāhi Rabbi-l-‘ālamīn, Lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāhi-l-‘Azīzi-l-Ḥakīm). Qāla: Fa ha’ulā’i li-Rabbī, fa mā lī? Qāla: Qul: (Allāhumma-ghfir lī, wa-rḥamnī, wa-hdinī, wa-rzuqnī).
duas.translation:
A desert Arab came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and said, "Teach me a word that I can say." The Prophet told him to say: "There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Who has no partner, Allah is the Great, the Most Great, and praise is to Allah in abundance, glory is to Allah, Lord of the worlds. There is no power and no might but by Allah the Mighty, the Wise." He said, "That is for my Lord, but what about me?" The Prophet (ﷺ) told him to say: "O Allah forgive me, and have mercy on me and guide me, and provide for me."

Reference: Muslim 4/2072, Abu Dawud reports the same Hadith with the addition: and when the Arab left, the Prophet (ﷺ) said: "He has filled his hands with goodness." 1/220.

duas.arabic: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا أَسْلَمَ عَلَّمَهُ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الصَّلاَةَ ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ بِهَؤُلاَءِ الْكَلِمَاتِ: (اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرِ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَعَافِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي)
duas.transliteration: Kāna-r-rajulu idhā aslama ‘allamahu-n-Nabiyyu ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa sallama-ṣ-ṣalāta thumma amarahu an yad‘uwa bi-ha’ulā’i-l-kalimāt: (Allāhumma-ghfir lī, wa-rḥamnī, wa-hdinī, wa ‘āfinī wa-rzuqnī).
duas.translation:
Whenever anyone accepted Islam, the Prophet (ﷺ) used to teach him how to pray then he would instruct him to invoke Allah with the following words: "O Allah forgive me, and have mercy on me and guide me and give me good health and provide for me."

Reference: Muslim 4/2073, and in one of Muslim's reports there is the addition: 'For these words combine [the goodness of] this world and the next.'

duas.arabic: إِنَّ أَفْضَلَ الدُّعَاءِ (الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ)، وَأَفْضَلَ الذِّكْرِ (لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ)
duas.transliteration: Inna afḍala ad-du‘ā’i (Al-ḥamdu lillāh), wa afḍala adh-dhikri (Lā ilāha illallāh).
duas.translation:
The most excellent invocation is: 'Praise is for Allah' (Alhamdulillah), and the most excellent words of remembrance are: 'There is none worthy of worship but Allah' (La ilaha illallah).

Reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/462, Ibn Majah 2/1249, and Al-Hakim who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 1/503. See Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 1/362.

duas.arabic: الباقيات الصالحات : (سبحان الله) و(الحمد لله )، و(لا إله إلا الله)، و(الله أكبر) ،و (لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله).
duas.transliteration: Al-bāqiyātuṣ-ṣāliḥāt: (Subḥānallāh), wa (Al-ḥamdu lillāh), wa (Lā ilāha illallāh), wa (Allāhu akbar), wa (Lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh).
duas.translation:
The good deeds which endure are: Glorified is Allah, all praise is for Allah, there is none worthy of worship but Allah, Allah is the Most Great, and there is no power and no might except by Allah.

Reference: Ahmad (no. 513) (Ahmad Shakir, ed.) and its chain of narration is authentic. See Majma'uz-Zawa'id 1/297. Ibn Hajar mentions it in Bulughul-Maram saying that Ibn Hibban and Al-Hakim considered it authentic.

chapters.title_english: Chapter: How the Prophet ﷺ made tasbeeh

duas.arabic: عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو رضي الله عنهما قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ النَّبيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَعْقِدُ التَّسْبِيحَ بِيَمِينِهِ.
duas.transliteration: ‘An ‘Abdillāhi bni ‘Amrin (raḍiyallāhu ‘anhumā) qāla: Ra’aytu an-Nabiyya (ṣallallāhu ‘alayhi wa-sallama) ya‘qidu at-tasbīḥa bi-yamīnihi.
duas.translation:
Abdullah bin 'Amr (RA) said: "I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) counting the glorification of his Lord on his right hand."

Reference: Abu Dawud with a different wording 2/81, and At-Tirmidhi 5/521. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami'As-Saghir 4/271 (no. 4865).

chapters.title_english: Chapter: Comprehensive types of good and manners

duas.arabic: إِذَا كَانَ جُنْحُ اللَّيْلِ – أَوْ أَمْسَيْتُم – فَـكُفُّوا صِبْيانَـكُم، فَإِنَّ الشَّيَاطِينَ تَنْتَشِرُ حِينَئِذٍ، فَإِذَا ذَهَبَ سَاعَةٌ مِنَ اللَّيلِ فَخَلُّوهُمْ، وَأَغْلِقُوا الأَبْوَابَ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ؛ فَإِنَّ الشَّيطَانَ لاَ يَفْتَحُ بَاباً مُغلَقاً، وَأَوْكُوا قِرَبَكُمْ، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَخَمِّرُوا آنِيَتَكُم، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَلَوْ أَنْ تَعْرُضُوا عَلَيْهَا شَيْئاً، وَأَطْفِئُوا مَصَابِيحَكُمْ.
duas.transliteration: Idhā kāna junḥu al-layli—aw amsaytum—fa-kuffū ṣibyānakum, fa-inna al-shayāṭīna tantashiru ḥīna’idhin, fa-idhā dhahaba sā‘atun min al-layli fa-khallūhum, wa-aghliqū al-abwāba wa-dhkurū isma Allāh, fa-inna al-shayṭāna lā yaftaḥu bāban mughlaqan, wa-awkū qirabakum wa-dhkurū isma Allāh, wa-khammirū āniyatakum wa-dhkurū isma Allāh—wa-law an ta‘ruḍū ‘alayhā shay’an—wa-atfi’ū maṣābīḥakum.
duas.translation: When night falls, restrain your children (from going out) because at such time the devils spread about. After a period of time has passed, let them be. Shut your doors and mention Allah’s name, for verily the devil does not open a shut door, tie up your water-skins and mention Allah’s name, cover your vessels with anything and mention Allah’s name and put out your lamps. Reference: Al-Bukhari with Al-Fath 10/88 and Muslim 3/1595.
